strategy: Every second round the Sphinx asks a riddle whose answer is a spell TYPE — cast that type to pass safely. Pack a Blade, a damage spell, a Trap, a Weakness, and a Heal so you always have the right answer.
Correct answers draw no response; wrong ones punish you (Orthrus, Power Nova, a +200% Feint on you, a protected Weakness, or he heals 10% of his health).
Wait for each new riddle before casting a different spell type — casting off-cue can answer wrong.
Passing is always safe and doesn't trigger any cheat, so pass if you lack the right card.
Use the Blade/Trap/Weakness riddles to build your setup, then land a big hit when a Damage riddle comes due (no resist, so any school works).
cheatNotes: Late wizards eat a Desert Hound — join on time.
Every 2nd round he asks a riddle answered by a spell type; answer right and nothing happens, answer wrong and he punishes you. Wait for each riddle before casting.
Pet may-cast talents can accidentally answer a riddle — watch your pet.
Passing never triggers the cheat, so pass if you lack the right card.
This riddle's answer is a Blade.
Answer it wrong and he casts Orthrus.
This riddle's answer is a Damage spell ('hurt me').
Wrong answer here brings a Shadow Power Nova.
This riddle's answer is a Trap.
Wrong answer gives you a +200% Shadow Feint (no self-trap for him).
This riddle's answer is a Weakness.
Wrong answer puts an Indemnity-protected Weakness on you.
This riddle's answer is a Heal.
Wrong answer heals him 10% of his total health.
